place: Leonia borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Leonia was named in 1865 by resident leaders after General Charles Lee, the Revolutionary War hero who inspired Fort Lee's name.
The Vreeland House, built in 1786 by Dirck Vreeland and expanded in 1815, joined the National Register of Historic Places in 1978.
The Civil War Drill Hall, first constructed in 1859, now houses the Players Guild of Leonia and stands as a preserved historic armory.
Leonia’s earliest settlement dates to 1668 when Samuel Edsall purchased 1,872 acres from Chief Oratam of the Hackensack Indians.
Founded in 1973, the Leonia Chamber Musicians Society brings professional classical concerts to the borough four times a year.
Leonia’s Northern Railroad station opened in 1859, marking the borough’s early connection to regional rail and mail distribution.
